faqs.question: What is the difficulty level of the Chobia Pass Trek?
faqs.answer: This route is rated difficult. The Chobia Pass Trek demands difficult effort across 8 day(s). Train with stair climbs and loaded walks; respect altitude above 16700 ft.

faqs.question: How long does the Chobia Pass Trek take?
faqs.answer: The standard package is 8D / 7N. Actual walking time varies with fitness, weather, and trail conditions.

faqs.question: What is the total trekking distance for Chobia Pass Trek?
faqs.answer: Expect roughly 75 km of walking across the itinerary (out-and-back or point-to-point depending on the route plan).

faqs.question: What is the maximum altitude on Chobia Pass Trek?
faqs.answer: The highest point is about 16,700 ft (5,090 m). Ascend steadily, hydrate, and tell your guide if you feel altitude-related symptoms on higher Dhauladhar routes.

faqs.question: Where does the Chobia Pass Trek start?
faqs.answer: The usual starting point is Bharmour / Kugti side. Arrive the evening before when the itinerary begins early.

faqs.question: When is the best time for Chobia Pass Trek?
faqs.answer: July–September weather windows only; avoid deep winter

faqs.question: Is mobile network available on Chobia Pass Trek?
faqs.answer: Expect patchy or zero network beyond the last town. BSNL sometimes works in remote valleys.

faqs.question: Do I need permits for Chobia Pass Trek?
faqs.answer: Inner forest / camping permits as required for the specific corridor; included when stated in package.

faqs.question: Is drinking water available on the Chobia Pass Trek trail?
faqs.answer: Carry adequate water from the last village; treat natural sources before drinking.

faqs.question: Where do we camp or stay on Chobia Pass Trek?
faqs.answer: Designated Chobia Pass camping zones Backup lower camp in bad weather

faqs.question: Can beginners attempt the Chobia Pass Trek?
faqs.answer: It depends on the grade. The Chobia Pass Trek demands difficult effort across 8 day(s). Train with stair climbs and loaded walks; respect altitude above 16700 ft. If you are new to the Himalayas, choose a guided departure and prepare with regular walks carrying a daypack.

faqs.question: How do I reach the trailhead for Chobia Pass Trek?
faqs.answer: Fly into Gaggal (DHM) or take a train to Pathankot, then road transfer to Bharmour–Lahaul. Local taxis and shared jeeps cover the final approach to Bharmour / Kugti side.

faqs.question: What should I pack for Chobia Pass Trek?
faqs.answer: Carry layered clothing, broken-in trekking shoes, rain shell, headlamp, sunscreen, personal medicines, and a power bank. A detailed packing list is provided on this page and with your booking confirmation.

faqs.question: Is Chobia Pass Trek safe for solo travellers?
faqs.answer: Guided group departures are the safest option for solo travellers in the Dhauladhar. Trails can be remote, weather changes fast, and night navigation is risky without local knowledge.

faqs.question: What is the cancellation and refund policy?
faqs.answer: Cancellations 45+ days before departure: 25% fee. 15–30 days: 50% fee. Within 0–15 days: 100% of the trek cost. For weather or government restrictions, we try to offer a viable alternative; cash refund may not be available. Approved refunds are credited to the original payment method in 10–12 working days.

faqs.question: Are meals included on the Chobia Pass Trek?
faqs.answer: Trek-day meals are included as listed in the package inclusions (typically vegetarian). Confirm dietary needs when you book so the kitchen team can plan.

faqs.question: What medical facilities are near Chobia Pass Trek?
faqs.answer: Basic clinics exist in Bharmour–Lahaul. For serious emergencies, evacuation aims for Civil / Zonal Hospital Dharamshala. Carry personal medication and inform us of any conditions before departure.

faqs.question: Is Chobia Pass Trek crowded?
faqs.answer: Chobia Pass sees far fewer groups than Triund — expect quieter camps and longer self-sufficiency.

faqs.question: Can I combine Chobia Pass with Triund?
faqs.answer: Many travellers pair easier Dharamshala walks with Triund or Kareri Lake on separate days. Ask us for a custom multi-trek plan.

title: Kugti Pass Trek

title: Jalsu Pass Trek
summary: Cross Jalsu Pass between Kangra and Chamba — shepherd highways, big meadow camps, and a storied trade-and-pilgrim route. From ₹12,999.
overview:
Jalsu Pass Trek is part of India Holiday Destinations’ Dharamshala and Dhauladhar collection. Cross Jalsu Pass between Kangra and Chamba — shepherd highways, big meadow camps, and a storied trade-and-pilgrim route.

Jalsu remains a living corridor between Kangra and Chamba: wide meadows, mule trains in season, and pass days that feel earned rather than rushed.

The itinerary usually starts at Baijnath / Billing–Rajgundha approaches and ends at Chamba side via Jalsu, spanning about 45 km across 5 day(s) with a high point near 11,800 ft. Graded challenging, it demands respect for Himachal weather, Leave No Trace camping, and honest self-assessment before you book.

Forest permissions: Follow Himachal Forest Department and local administration rules for camping, fire, and waste.

faqs.question: What is the difficulty level of the Jalsu Pass Trek?
faqs.answer: This route is rated challenging. The Jalsu Pass Trek demands challenging effort across 5 day(s). Train with stair climbs and loaded walks; respect altitude above 11800 ft.

faqs.question: How long does the Jalsu Pass Trek take?
faqs.answer: The standard package is 5D / 4N. Actual walking time varies with fitness, weather, and trail conditions.

faqs.question: What is the total trekking distance for Jalsu Pass Trek?
faqs.answer: Expect roughly 45 km of walking across the itinerary (out-and-back or point-to-point depending on the route plan).

faqs.question: What is the maximum altitude on Jalsu Pass Trek?
faqs.answer: The highest point is about 11,800 ft (3,596 m). Ascend steadily, hydrate, and tell your guide if you feel altitude-related symptoms on higher Dhauladhar routes.

faqs.question: Where does the Jalsu Pass Trek start?
faqs.answer: The usual starting point is Baijnath / Billing–Rajgundha approaches. Arrive the evening before when the itinerary begins early.

faqs.question: When is the best time for Jalsu Pass Trek?
faqs.answer: April–June and September–November for most stable Dhauladhar weather

faqs.question: Is mobile network available on Jalsu Pass Trek?
faqs.answer: Expect patchy or zero network beyond the last town. BSNL sometimes works in remote valleys.

faqs.question: Do I need permits for Jalsu Pass Trek?
faqs.answer: Inner forest / camping permits as required for the specific corridor; included when stated in package.

faqs.question: Is drinking water available on the Jalsu Pass Trek trail?
faqs.answer: Carry adequate water from the last village; treat natural sources before drinking.

faqs.question: Where do we camp or stay on Jalsu Pass Trek?
faqs.answer: Designated Jalsu Pass camping zones Backup lower camp in bad weather

faqs.question: Can beginners attempt the Jalsu Pass Trek?
faqs.answer: It depends on the grade. The Jalsu Pass Trek demands challenging effort across 5 day(s). Train with stair climbs and loaded walks; respect altitude above 11800 ft. If you are new to the Himalayas, choose a guided departure and prepare with regular walks carrying a daypack.

faqs.question: How do I reach the trailhead for Jalsu Pass Trek?
faqs.answer: Fly into Gaggal (DHM) or take a train to Pathankot, then road transfer to Kangra–Chamba. Local taxis and shared jeeps cover the final approach to Baijnath / Billing–Rajgundha approaches.

faqs.question: What should I pack for Jalsu Pass Trek?
faqs.answer: Carry layered clothing, broken-in trekking shoes, rain shell, headlamp, sunscreen, personal medicines, and a power bank. A detailed packing list is provided on this page and with your booking confirmation.

faqs.question: Is Jalsu Pass Trek safe for solo travellers?
faqs.answer: Guided group departures are the safest option for solo travellers in the Dhauladhar. Trails can be remote, weather changes fast, and night navigation is risky without local knowledge.

faqs.question: What is the cancellation and refund policy?
faqs.answer: Cancellations 45+ days before departure: 25% fee. 15–30 days: 50% fee. Within 0–15 days: 100% of the trek cost. For weather or government restrictions, we try to offer a viable alternative; cash refund may not be available. Approved refunds are credited to the original payment method in 10–12 working days.

faqs.question: Are meals included on the Jalsu Pass Trek?
faqs.answer: Trek-day meals are included as listed in the package inclusions (typically vegetarian). Confirm dietary needs when you book so the kitchen team can plan.

faqs.question: What medical facilities are near Jalsu Pass Trek?
faqs.answer: Basic clinics exist in Kangra–Chamba. For serious emergencies, evacuation aims for Civil / Zonal Hospital Dharamshala. Carry personal medication and inform us of any conditions before departure.

faqs.question: Is Jalsu Pass Trek crowded?
faqs.answer: Jalsu Pass sees far fewer groups than Triund — expect quieter camps and longer self-sufficiency.

faqs.question: Can I combine Jalsu Pass with Triund?
faqs.answer: Many travellers pair easier Dharamshala walks with Triund or Kareri Lake on separate days. Ask us for a custom multi-trek plan.

title: Thamsar Pass Trek
summary: One of Himachal’s great wilderness crossings via Thamsar Pass toward Bara Bhangal — remote, long, and unforgettable. From ₹29,999.
overview:
Thamsar Pass Trek is part of India Holiday Destinations’ Dharamshala and Dhauladhar collection. One of Himachal’s great wilderness crossings via Thamsar Pass toward Bara Bhangal — remote, long, and unforgettable.

Thamsar is wilderness cinema — multi-day approaches, sparse villages, and the legendary pull of Bara Bhangal on the far side.

The itinerary usually starts at Rajgundha / Bara Bhangal approaches and ends at Thamsar Pass & beyond, spanning about 85 km across 9 day(s) with a high point near 16,200 ft. Graded difficult, it demands respect for Himachal weather, Leave No Trace camping, and honest self-assessment before you book.

Best time to visit: July–September weather windows only; avoid deep winter

Flora along the Thamsar Pass corridor: Regional mix of chir pine, ban oak, rhododendron, deodar, and high-altitude scrub depending on elevation band.

Wildlife notes for Thamsar Pass Trek: Himalayan birds (monal, griffons), langurs in forests, and occasional goral on steep grass — observe from distance.

Photography spots: Thamsar Pass main viewpoint; Valley light at golden hour; Camp ridgeline panoramas; Village / temple cultural frames.

Camping locations: Designated Thamsar Pass camping zones; Backup lower camp in bad weather.

Water sources: Carry adequate water from the last village; treat natural sources before drinking.

Network availability: Expect patchy or zero network beyond the last town. BSNL sometimes works in remote valleys.

Electricity availability: No reliable charging on trail — carry power banks.

ATM availability: Last reliable ATMs in Dharamshala, McLeod Ganj, Palampur, Baijnath, or Bharmour depending on approach.

Medical facilities: Basic trail first aid; nearest substantial care in Dharamshala / regional district hospitals.

Permits required: Inner forest / camping permits as required for the specific corridor; included when stated in package.

Forest permissions: Follow Himachal Forest Department and local administration rules for camping, fire, and waste.

Safety on Thamsar Pass: Stay on marked trails and follow your trek leader’s briefings. Turn back early if weather deteriorates — Dhauladhar storms build quickly. Never wander alone after dark near cliffs, snow patches, or stream crossings. Always follow your trek leader’s brief for this specific route.

Travel tips: Arrive in Bara Bhangal a night early when mornings start at the trailhead. Download offline maps; do not rely on trail network. Carry cash for village tea shops and emergency transport.

Responsible tourism on Thamsar Pass Trek: Leave no trace — pack out all non-biodegradable waste. Use designated camping zones and toilet practices advised by your guide. Support local porters, mules, and homestays fairly.

Nearby attractions from this trailhead: McLeod Ganj, Bhagsu Nag, Kangra Fort, Bir Billing, Masroor Temples.

Nearby treks: Rajgundha Trek, Bir Billing to Rajgundha Trek, Barot Valley Trek, Snowline Laka Trek.

Child policy for Thamsar Pass Trek: 16+ experienced preferred. Children are welcome on easier Dhauladhar trails when accompanied by a parent or guardian. Minimum age depends on the route (typically 8+ for short day hikes, 12+ for overnight alpine camps). Share ages at booking for route-specific advice.

Private departures: Private Thamsar Pass Trek departures can be arranged for families and corporate groups subject to guide availability.

Group discount: Group discount available for 6+ travellers on the same fixed departure.

Booking and cancellation policies for India Holiday Destinations packages apply — see FAQs on this page for the current fee schedule.

Things to carry beyond the packing list: Layered clothing; Rain shell; Headlamp; Personal medicines; Reusable water bottle.

Things not to carry: Single-use plastics beyond reusable bottles; Heavy jeans or cotton-heavy clothing for multi-day wet weather; Unnecessary valuables and excess jewellery; Alcohol and recreational drugs.

title: Rajgundha Trek
summary: Meadow trek from the Bir Billing paragliding hills into quiet Rajgundha — pine forests, Gaddi culture, and wide Dhauladhar views. From ₹5,499.

faqs.question: How do I reach the trailhead for Rajgundha Trek?

faqs.question: What should I pack for Rajgundha Trek?
faqs.answer: Carry layered clothing, broken-in trekking shoes, rain shell, headlamp, sunscreen, personal medicines, and a power bank. A detailed packing list is provided on this page and with your booking confirmation.

faqs.question: Is Rajgundha Trek safe for solo travellers?
faqs.answer: Guided group departures are the safest option for solo travellers in the Dhauladhar. Trails can be remote, weather changes fast, and night navigation is risky without local knowledge.

faqs.question: What is the cancellation and refund policy?
faqs.answer: Cancellations 45+ days before departure: 25% fee. 15–30 days: 50% fee. Within 0–15 days: 100% of the trek cost. For weather or government restrictions, we try to offer a viable alternative; cash refund may not be available. Approved refunds are credited to the original payment method in 10–12 working days.

faqs.question: Are meals included on the Rajgundha Trek?
faqs.answer: Trek-day meals are included as listed in the package inclusions (typically vegetarian). Confirm dietary needs when you book so the kitchen team can plan.

faqs.question: What medical facilities are near Rajgundha Trek?
faqs.answer: Basic clinics exist in Bir Billing. For serious emergencies, evacuation aims for Civil / Zonal Hospital Dharamshala. Carry personal medication and inform us of any conditions before departure.

faqs.question: Is Rajgundha Trek crowded?
faqs.answer: Rajgundha sees far fewer groups than Triund — expect quieter camps and longer self-sufficiency.

faqs.question: Can I combine Rajgundha with Triund?
faqs.answer: Many travellers pair easier Dharamshala walks with Triund or Kareri Lake on separate days. Ask us for a custom multi-trek plan.

seo.description: Meadow trek from the Bir Billing paragliding hills into quiet Rajgundha — pine forests, Gaddi culture, and wide Dhauladhar views. From ₹5,499.

title: Bir Billing to Rajgundha Trek
summary: The popular Bir Billing to Rajgundha walk — paraglider skies behind you, forest trails ahead, and a riverside village camp. From ₹3,999.
overview:
Bir Billing to Rajgundha Trek is part of India Holiday Destinations’ Dharamshala and Dhauladhar collection. The popular Bir Billing to Rajgundha walk — paraglider skies behind you, forest trails ahead, and a riverside village camp.

The Billing–Rajgundha link is the weekend favourite: start among take-off ramps, finish beside a quiet Gaddi settlement.

The itinerary usually starts at Billing ridge and ends at Rajgundha, spanning about 18 km across 2 day(s) with a high point near 9,800 ft. Graded moderate, it demands respect for Himachal weather, Leave No Trace camping, and honest self-assessment before you book.

Best time to visit: April–June and September–November for most stable Dhauladhar weather

Flora along the Bir to Rajgundha corridor: Regional mix of chir pine, ban oak, rhododendron, deodar, and high-altitude scrub depending on elevation band.

Wildlife notes for Bir Billing to Rajgundha Trek: Himalayan birds (monal, griffons), langurs in forests, and occasional goral on steep grass — observe from distance.

Photography spots: Bir to Rajgundha main viewpoint; Valley light at golden hour; Camp ridgeline panoramas; Village / temple cultural frames.

Camping locations: Designated Bir to Rajgundha camping zones; Backup lower camp in bad weather.

Water sources: Carry adequate water from the last village; treat natural sources before drinking.

Network availability: Expect patchy or zero network beyond the last town. BSNL sometimes works in remote valleys.

Electricity availability: No reliable charging on trail — carry power banks.

ATM availability: Last reliable ATMs in Dharamshala, McLeod Ganj, Palampur, Baijnath, or Bharmour depending on approach.

Medical facilities: Basic trail first aid; nearest substantial care in Dharamshala / regional district hospitals.

Permits required: Inner forest / camping permits as required for the specific corridor; included when stated in package.

Forest permissions: Follow Himachal Forest Department and local administration rules for camping, fire, and waste.

Safety on Bir to Rajgundha: Stay on marked trails and follow your trek leader’s briefings. Turn back early if weather deteriorates — Dhauladhar storms build quickly. Never wander alone after dark near cliffs, snow patches, or stream crossings. Always follow your trek leader’s brief for this specific route.

Travel tips: Arrive in Bir Billing a night early when mornings start at the trailhead. Download offline maps; do not rely on trail network. Carry cash for village tea shops and emergency transport.

Responsible tourism on Bir Billing to Rajgundha Trek: Leave no trace — pack out all non-biodegradable waste. Use designated camping zones and toilet practices advised by your guide. Support local porters, mules, and homestays fairly.

Nearby attractions from this trailhead: McLeod Ganj, Bhagsu Nag, Kangra Fort, Bir Billing, Masroor Temples.

Nearby treks: Barot Valley Trek, Chhota Bhanghal Trek, Bara Bhangal Trek, Kareri Lake Trek.

Child policy for Bir Billing to Rajgundha Trek: 12+ with fitness. Children are welcome on easier Dhauladhar trails when accompanied by a parent or guardian. Minimum age depends on the route (typically 8+ for short day hikes, 12+ for overnight alpine camps). Share ages at booking for route-specific advice.

Private departures: Private Bir Billing to Rajgundha Trek departures can be arranged for families and corporate groups subject to guide availability.

Group discount: Group discount available for 6+ travellers on the same fixed departure.

Booking and cancellation policies for India Holiday Destinations packages apply — see FAQs on this page for the current fee schedule.

Things to carry beyond the packing list: Layered clothing; Rain shell; Headlamp; Personal medicines; Reusable water bottle.

Things not to carry: Single-use plastics beyond reusable bottles; Heavy jeans or cotton-heavy clothing for multi-day wet weather; Unnecessary valuables and excess jewellery; Alcohol and recreational drugs.

title: Chhota Bhanghal Trek
summary: Explore Chhota Bhanghal’s lesser-known valleys — village trails, river crossings, and authentic mid-Himalayan culture. From ₹8,999.
overview:
Chhota Bhanghal Trek is part of India Holiday Destinations’ Dharamshala and Dhauladhar collection. Explore Chhota Bhanghal’s lesser-known valleys — village trails, river crossings, and authentic mid-Himalayan culture.

Chhota Bhanghal reveals mid-Himalayan village life without the Instagram density of McLeod Ganj — rivers, terraces, and honest walking days.

The itinerary usually starts at Barot / Poling approaches and ends at Chhota Bhanghal villages, spanning about 35 km across 4 day(s) with a high point near 10,500 ft. Graded moderate, it demands respect for Himachal weather, Leave No Trace camping, and honest self-assessment before you book.

Best time to visit: April–June and September–November for most stable Dhauladhar weather

Flora along the Chhota Bhanghal corridor: Regional mix of chir pine, ban oak, rhododendron, deodar, and high-altitude scrub depending on elevation band.

Wildlife notes for Chhota Bhanghal Trek: Himalayan birds (monal, griffons), langurs in forests, and occasional goral on steep grass — observe from distance.

Photography spots: Chhota Bhanghal main viewpoint; Valley light at golden hour; Camp ridgeline panoramas; Village / temple cultural frames.

Camping locations: Designated Chhota Bhanghal camping zones; Backup lower camp in bad weather.

Water sources: Carry adequate water from the last village; treat natural sources before drinking.

Network availability: Expect patchy or zero network beyond the last town. BSNL sometimes works in remote valleys.

Electricity availability: No reliable charging on trail — carry power banks.

ATM availability: Last reliable ATMs in Dharamshala, McLeod Ganj, Palampur, Baijnath, or Bharmour depending on approach.

Medical facilities: Basic trail first aid; nearest substantial care in Dharamshala / regional district hospitals.

Permits required: Inner forest / camping permits as required for the specific corridor; included when stated in package.

Forest permissions: Follow Himachal Forest Department and local administration rules for camping, fire, and waste.

Safety on Chhota Bhanghal: Stay on marked trails and follow your trek leader’s briefings. Turn back early if weather deteriorates — Dhauladhar storms build quickly. Never wander alone after dark near cliffs, snow patches, or stream crossings. Always follow your trek leader’s brief for this specific route.

Travel tips: Arrive in Barot–Bhanghal a night early when mornings start at the trailhead. Download offline maps; do not rely on trail network. Carry cash for village tea shops and emergency transport.

Responsible tourism on Chhota Bhanghal Trek: Leave no trace — pack out all non-biodegradable waste. Use designated camping zones and toilet practices advised by your guide. Support local porters, mules, and homestays fairly.

Nearby attractions from this trailhead: McLeod Ganj, Bhagsu Nag, Kangra Fort, Bir Billing, Masroor Temples.

Nearby treks: Bara Bhangal Trek, Arthur's Seat Trek, Camel Peak Trek, Triund Trek.

Child policy for Chhota Bhanghal Trek: 12+ with fitness. Children are welcome on easier Dhauladhar trails when accompanied by a parent or guardian. Minimum age depends on the route (typically 8+ for short day hikes, 12+ for overnight alpine camps). Share ages at booking for route-specific advice.

Private departures: Private Chhota Bhanghal Trek departures can be arranged for families and corporate groups subject to guide availability.

Group discount: Group discount available for 6+ travellers on the same fixed departure.

Booking and cancellation policies for India Holiday Destinations packages apply — see FAQs on this page for the current fee schedule.

Things to carry beyond the packing list: Layered clothing; Rain shell; Headlamp; Personal medicines; Reusable water bottle.

Things not to carry: Single-use plastics beyond reusable bottles; Heavy jeans or cotton-heavy clothing for multi-day wet weather; Unnecessary valuables and excess jewellery; Alcohol and recreational drugs.

Fixed departure calendar and live pricing shown in the booking panel remain editable from CMS.

faqs.question: What is the difficulty level of the Chhota Bhanghal Trek?
faqs.answer: This route is rated moderate. The Chhota Bhanghal Trek demands moderate effort across 4 day(s). Train with stair climbs and loaded walks; respect altitude above 10500 ft.

faqs.question: How long does the Chhota Bhanghal Trek take?
faqs.answer: The standard package is 4D / 3N. Actual walking time varies with fitness, weather, and trail conditions.

faqs.question: What is the total trekking distance for Chhota Bhanghal Trek?
faqs.answer: Expect roughly 35 km of walking across the itinerary (out-and-back or point-to-point depending on the route plan).

faqs.question: What is the maximum altitude on Chhota Bhanghal Trek?
faqs.answer: The highest point is about 10,500 ft (3,200 m). Ascend steadily, hydrate, and tell your guide if you feel altitude-related symptoms on higher Dhauladhar routes.

faqs.question: Where does the Chhota Bhanghal Trek start?
faqs.answer: The usual starting point is Barot / Poling approaches. Arrive the evening before when the itinerary begins early.

faqs.question: When is the best time for Chhota Bhanghal Trek?
faqs.answer: April–June and September–November for most stable Dhauladhar weather

faqs.question: Is mobile network available on Chhota Bhanghal Trek?
faqs.answer: Expect patchy or zero network beyond the last town. BSNL sometimes works in remote valleys.

faqs.question: Do I need permits for Chhota Bhanghal Trek?
faqs.answer: Inner forest / camping permits as required for the specific corridor; included when stated in package.

faqs.question: Is drinking water available on the Chhota Bhanghal Trek trail?
faqs.answer: Carry adequate water from the last village; treat natural sources before drinking.

faqs.question: Where do we camp or stay on Chhota Bhanghal Trek?
faqs.answer: Designated Chhota Bhanghal camping zones Backup lower camp in bad weather

faqs.question: Can beginners attempt the Chhota Bhanghal Trek?
faqs.answer: It depends on the grade. The Chhota Bhanghal Trek demands moderate effort across 4 day(s). Train with stair climbs and loaded walks; respect altitude above 10500 ft. If you are new to the Himalayas, choose a guided departure and prepare with regular walks carrying a daypack.

faqs.question: How do I reach the trailhead for Chhota Bhanghal Trek?
faqs.answer: Fly into Gaggal (DHM) or take a train to Pathankot, then road transfer to Barot–Bhanghal. Local taxis and shared jeeps cover the final approach to Barot / Poling approaches.

faqs.question: What should I pack for Chhota Bhanghal Trek?
faqs.answer: Carry layered clothing, broken-in trekking shoes, rain shell, headlamp, sunscreen, personal medicines, and a power bank. A detailed packing list is provided on this page and with your booking confirmation.

faqs.question: Is Chhota Bhanghal Trek safe for solo travellers?
faqs.answer: Guided group departures are the safest option for solo travellers in the Dhauladhar. Trails can be remote, weather changes fast, and night navigation is risky without local knowledge.

faqs.question: What is the cancellation and refund policy?
faqs.answer: Cancellations 45+ days before departure: 25% fee. 15–30 days: 50% fee. Within 0–15 days: 100% of the trek cost. For weather or government restrictions, we try to offer a viable alternative; cash refund may not be available. Approved refunds are credited to the original payment method in 10–12 working days.

faqs.question: Are meals included on the Chhota Bhanghal Trek?
faqs.answer: Trek-day meals are included as listed in the package inclusions (typically vegetarian). Confirm dietary needs when you book so the kitchen team can plan.

faqs.question: What medical facilities are near Chhota Bhanghal Trek?
faqs.answer: Basic clinics exist in Barot–Bhanghal. For serious emergencies, evacuation aims for Civil / Zonal Hospital Dharamshala. Carry personal medication and inform us of any conditions before departure.

faqs.question: Is Chhota Bhanghal Trek crowded?
faqs.answer: Chhota Bhanghal sees far fewer groups than Triund — expect quieter camps and longer self-sufficiency.

faqs.question: Can I combine Chhota Bhanghal with Triund?
faqs.answer: Many travellers pair easier Dharamshala walks with Triund or Kareri Lake on separate days. Ask us for a custom multi-trek plan.

title: Bara Bhangal Trek

title: Arthur's Seat Trek
summary: Quiet viewpoint hike to Arthur’s Seat above the McLeod–Dharamkot belt — forest shade and a classic Kangra Valley overlook. From ₹699.
overview:
Arthur's Seat Trek is part of India Holiday Destinations’ Dharamshala and Dhauladhar collection. Quiet viewpoint hike to Arthur’s Seat above the McLeod–Dharamkot belt — forest shade and a classic Kangra Valley overlook.

Arthur’s Seat offers a classic Kangra overlook without heavy packs — forest approaches and a wide valley reveal at the top.

The itinerary usually starts at McLeod Ganj / Dharamkot trails and ends at Arthur's Seat viewpoint, spanning about 5 km across 1 day(s) with a high point near 7,200 ft. Graded easy, it demands respect for Himachal weather, Leave No Trace camping, and honest self-assessment before you book.

Best time to visit: April–June and September–November for most stable Dhauladhar weather

Flora along the Arthur's Seat corridor: Regional mix of chir pine, ban oak, rhododendron, deodar, and high-altitude scrub depending on elevation band.

Photography spots: Arthur's Seat main viewpoint; Valley light at golden hour; Camp ridgeline panoramas; Village / temple cultural frames.

Camping locations: Not applicable — day trek.

title: Camel Peak Trek
summary: Ridge trek toward Camel Peak profiles on the Dhauladhar skyline — photographic rock shapes and airy meadow walks. From ₹2,499.
overview:
Camel Peak Trek is part of India Holiday Destinations’ Dharamshala and Dhauladhar collection. Ridge trek toward Camel Peak profiles on the Dhauladhar skyline — photographic rock shapes and airy meadow walks.

Camel Peak’s silhouette from the Triund belt is unforgettable; the ridge walk toward it rewards careful footing and clear mornings.

The itinerary usually starts at Triund belt approaches and ends at Camel Peak viewpoint, spanning about 14 km across 2 day(s) with a high point near 9,800 ft. Graded moderate, it demands respect for Himachal weather, Leave No Trace camping, and honest self-assessment before you book.

faqs.question: Do I need permits for Rifle Horn Trek?
faqs.answer: Inner forest / camping permits as required for the specific corridor; included when stated in package.

faqs.question: Is drinking water available on the Rifle Horn Trek trail?
faqs.answer: Carry adequate water from the last village; treat natural sources before drinking.

faqs.question: Where do we camp or stay on Rifle Horn Trek?
faqs.answer: Designated Rifle Horn camping zones Backup lower camp in bad weather

faqs.question: Can beginners attempt the Rifle Horn Trek?
faqs.answer: It depends on the grade. The Rifle Horn Trek demands challenging effort across 3 day(s). Train with stair climbs and loaded walks; respect altitude above 12000 ft. If you are new to the Himalayas, choose a guided departure and prepare with regular walks carrying a daypack.

faqs.question: How do I reach the trailhead for Rifle Horn Trek?
faqs.answer: Fly into Gaggal (DHM) or take a train to Pathankot, then road transfer to Dharamshala. Local taxis and shared jeeps cover the final approach to Upper Dharamshala alpine approaches.

faqs.question: What should I pack for Rifle Horn Trek?
faqs.answer: Carry layered clothing, broken-in trekking shoes, rain shell, headlamp, sunscreen, personal medicines, and a power bank. A detailed packing list is provided on this page and with your booking confirmation.

faqs.question: Is Rifle Horn Trek safe for solo travellers?
faqs.answer: Guided group departures are the safest option for solo travellers in the Dhauladhar. Trails can be remote, weather changes fast, and night navigation is risky without local knowledge.

faqs.question: What is the cancellation and refund policy?
faqs.answer: Cancellations 45+ days before departure: 25% fee. 15–30 days: 50% fee. Within 0–15 days: 100% of the trek cost. For weather or government restrictions, we try to offer a viable alternative; cash refund may not be available. Approved refunds are credited to the original payment method in 10–12 working days.

faqs.question: Are meals included on the Rifle Horn Trek?
faqs.answer: Trek-day meals are included as listed in the package inclusions (typically vegetarian). Confirm dietary needs when you book so the kitchen team can plan.

faqs.question: What medical facilities are near Rifle Horn Trek?
faqs.answer: Basic clinics exist in Dharamshala. For serious emergencies, evacuation aims for Civil / Zonal Hospital Dharamshala. Carry personal medication and inform us of any conditions before departure.

faqs.question: Is Rifle Horn Trek crowded?
faqs.answer: Rifle Horn sees far fewer groups than Triund — expect quieter camps and longer self-sufficiency.

faqs.question: Can I combine Rifle Horn with Triund?
faqs.answer: Many travellers pair easier Dharamshala walks with Triund or Kareri Lake on separate days. Ask us for a custom multi-trek plan.

title: Ilaqa Got Trek
summary: High pasture trek to Ilaqa Got — Gaddi herding grounds, wildflowers in season, and quiet camping under granite walls. From ₹5,999.
overview:
Ilaqa Got Trek is part of India Holiday Destinations’ Dharamshala and Dhauladhar collection. High pasture trek to Ilaqa Got — Gaddi herding grounds, wildflowers in season, and quiet camping under granite walls.

Ilaqa Got is high pasture poetry: seasonal flowers, herding camps, and granite walls that feel close enough to touch.

The itinerary usually starts at Kareri / Dhauladhar pasture approaches and ends at Ilaqa Got meadow, spanning about 22 km across 3 day(s) with a high point near 11,200 ft. Graded moderate, it demands respect for Himachal weather, Leave No Trace camping, and honest self-assessment before you book.

Best time to visit: April–June and September–November for most stable Dhauladhar weather

Flora along the Ilaqa Got corridor: Regional mix of chir pine, ban oak, rhododendron, deodar, and high-altitude scrub depending on elevation band.

Wildlife notes for Ilaqa Got Trek: Himalayan birds (monal, griffons), langurs in forests, and occasional goral on steep grass — observe from distance.

Photography spots: Ilaqa Got main viewpoint; Valley light at golden hour; Camp ridgeline panoramas; Village / temple cultural frames.

Camping locations: Designated Ilaqa Got camping zones; Backup lower camp in bad weather.

Water sources: Carry adequate water from the last village; treat natural sources before drinking.

Network availability: Expect patchy or zero network beyond the last town. BSNL sometimes works in remote valleys.

Electricity availability: No reliable charging on trail — carry power banks.

ATM availability: Last reliable ATMs in Dharamshala, McLeod Ganj, Palampur, Baijnath, or Bharmour depending on approach.

Medical facilities: Basic trail first aid; nearest substantial care in Dharamshala / regional district hospitals.

Permits required: Inner forest / camping permits as required for the specific corridor; included when stated in package.

Forest permissions: Follow Himachal Forest Department and local administration rules for camping, fire, and waste.

Safety on Ilaqa Got: Stay on marked trails and follow your trek leader’s briefings. Turn back early if weather deteriorates — Dhauladhar storms build quickly. Never wander alone after dark near cliffs, snow patches, or stream crossings. Always follow your trek leader’s brief for this specific route.

Travel tips: Arrive in Dharamshala a night early when mornings start at the trailhead. Download offline maps; do not rely on trail network. Carry cash for village tea shops and emergency transport.

Responsible tourism on Ilaqa Got Trek: Leave no trace — pack out all non-biodegradable waste. Use designated camping zones and toilet practices advised by your guide. Support local porters, mules, and homestays fairly.

Nearby attractions from this trailhead: McLeod Ganj, Bhagsu Nag, Kangra Fort, Bir Billing, Masroor Temples.

Nearby treks: Reoti Trek, Tatwani Trek, Kunal Pathri Trek, Snowline Laka Trek.

Child policy for Ilaqa Got Trek: 12+ with fitness. Children are welcome on easier Dhauladhar trails when accompanied by a parent or guardian. Minimum age depends on the route (typically 8+ for short day hikes, 12+ for overnight alpine camps). Share ages at booking for route-specific advice.

Private departures: Private Ilaqa Got Trek departures can be arranged for families and corporate groups subject to guide availability.

Group discount: Group discount available for 6+ travellers on the same fixed departure.

Booking and cancellation policies for India Holiday Destinations packages apply — see FAQs on this page for the current fee schedule.

Things to carry beyond the packing list: Layered clothing; Rain shell; Headlamp; Personal medicines; Reusable water bottle.

Things not to carry: Single-use plastics beyond reusable bottles; Heavy jeans or cotton-heavy clothing for multi-day wet weather; Unnecessary valuables and excess jewellery; Alcohol and recreational drugs.

faqs.question: Is mobile network available on Tatwani Trek?

faqs.question: How do I reach the trailhead for Tatwani Trek?
faqs.answer: Fly into Gaggal (DHM) or take a train to Pathankot, then road transfer to Kangra. Local taxis and shared jeeps cover the final approach to Tatwani roadhead.

faqs.question: What should I pack for Tatwani Trek?
faqs.answer: Carry layered clothing, broken-in trekking shoes, rain shell, headlamp, sunscreen, personal medicines, and a power bank. A detailed packing list is provided on this page and with your booking confirmation.

faqs.question: Is Tatwani Trek safe for solo travellers?
faqs.answer: Guided group departures are the safest option for solo travellers in the Dhauladhar. Trails can be remote, weather changes fast, and night navigation is risky without local knowledge.

faqs.question: What is the cancellation and refund policy?
faqs.answer: Cancellations 45+ days before departure: 25% fee. 15–30 days: 50% fee. Within 0–15 days: 100% of the trek cost. For weather or government restrictions, we try to offer a viable alternative; cash refund may not be available. Approved refunds are credited to the original payment method in 10–12 working days.

faqs.question: Are meals included on the Tatwani Trek?
faqs.answer: Trek-day meals are included as listed in the package inclusions (typically vegetarian). Confirm dietary needs when you book so the kitchen team can plan.

faqs.question: What medical facilities are near Tatwani Trek?
faqs.answer: Basic clinics exist in Kangra. For serious emergencies, evacuation aims for Civil / Zonal Hospital Dharamshala. Carry personal medication and inform us of any conditions before departure.

faqs.question: Is Tatwani Trek crowded?
faqs.answer: Popular sections near towns can be busy on weekends; early starts help.

faqs.question: Can I combine Tatwani with Triund?
faqs.answer: Many travellers pair easier Dharamshala walks with Triund or Kareri Lake on separate days. Ask us for a custom multi-trek plan.

title: Kunal Pathri Trek
summary: Sacred hill walk to Kunal Pathri Temple — pine forests, local pilgrims, and a serene half-day outing from Dharamshala. From ₹799.
overview:
Kunal Pathri Trek is part of India Holiday Destinations’ Dharamshala and Dhauladhar collection. Sacred hill walk to Kunal Pathri Temple — pine forests, local pilgrims, and a serene half-day outing from Dharamshala.

Kunal Pathri’s temple hill is pine-scented and locally loved — a half-day cultural climb when you want calm over conquest.

The itinerary usually starts at Kunal Pathri Temple approach and ends at Kunal Pathri, spanning about 5 km across 1 day(s) with a high point near 6,500 ft. Graded easy, it demands respect for Himachal weather, Leave No Trace camping, and honest self-assessment before you book.

Best time to visit: April–June and September–November for most stable Dhauladhar weather

Flora along the Kunal Pathri corridor: Regional mix of chir pine, ban oak, rhododendron, deodar, and high-altitude scrub depending on elevation band.

Wildlife notes for Kunal Pathri Trek: Himalayan birds (monal, griffons), langurs in forests, and occasional goral on steep grass — observe from distance.

Photography spots: Kunal Pathri main viewpoint; Valley light at golden hour; Camp ridgeline panoramas; Village / temple cultural frames.

Camping locations: Not applicable — day trek.

Water sources: Carry adequate water from the last village; treat natural sources before drinking.

Network availability: Expect patchy or zero network beyond the last town. BSNL sometimes works in remote valleys.

Electricity availability: Charge devices in town before starting.

ATM availability: Last reliable ATMs in Dharamshala, McLeod Ganj, Palampur, Baijnath, or Bharmour depending on approach.

Medical facilities: Basic trail first aid; nearest substantial care in Dharamshala / regional district hospitals.

Permits required: Inner forest / camping permits as required for the specific corridor; included when stated in package.

Forest permissions: Follow Himachal Forest Department and local administration rules for camping, fire, and waste.

Safety on Kunal Pathri: Stay on marked trails and follow your trek leader’s briefings. Turn back early if weather deteriorates — Dhauladhar storms build quickly. Never wander alone after dark near cliffs, snow patches, or stream crossings. Always follow your trek leader’s brief for this specific route.

Travel tips: Arrive in Dharamshala a night early when mornings start at the trailhead. Download offline maps; do not rely on trail network. Carry cash for village tea shops and emergency transport.

Responsible tourism on Kunal Pathri Trek: Leave no trace — pack out all non-biodegradable waste. Use designated camping zones and toilet practices advised by your guide. Support local porters, mules, and homestays fairly.

Nearby attractions from this trailhead: McLeod Ganj, Bhagsu Nag, Kangra Fort, Bir Billing, Masroor Temples.

Nearby treks: Chamunda Trek, Aghanjar Mahadev Trek, Galu Devi Trek, Snowline Laka Trek.

Child policy for Kunal Pathri Trek: 8+ with guardians. Children are welcome on easier Dhauladhar trails when accompanied by a parent or guardian. Minimum age depends on the route (typically 8+ for short day hikes, 12+ for overnight alpine camps). Share ages at booking for route-specific advice.

Private departures: Private Kunal Pathri Trek departures can be arranged for families and corporate groups subject to guide availability.

Group discount: Group discount available for 6+ travellers on the same fixed departure.

Booking and cancellation policies for India Holiday Destinations packages apply — see FAQs on this page for the current fee schedule.

Things to carry beyond the packing list: Layered clothing; Rain shell; Headlamp; Personal medicines; Reusable water bottle.

Things not to carry: Single-use plastics beyond reusable bottles; Heavy jeans or cotton-heavy clothing for multi-day wet weather; Unnecessary valuables and excess jewellery; Alcohol and recreational drugs.

title: Aghanjar Mahadev Trek
summary: Forest walk to Aghanjar Mahadev — ancient Shiva shrine set among trees, ideal for a quiet cultural half-day near the airport belt. From ₹699.
overview:
Aghanjar Mahadev Trek is part of India Holiday Destinations’ Dharamshala and Dhauladhar collection. Forest walk to Aghanjar Mahadev — ancient Shiva shrine set among trees, ideal for a quiet cultural half-day near the airport belt.

Aghanjar Mahadev hides an old Shiva shrine in forest calm near the Gaggal belt — ideal when flight timings leave you a free half day.

The itinerary usually starts at Near Gaggal / Kangra approaches and ends at Aghanjar Mahadev Temple, spanning about 5 km across 1 day(s) with a high point near 4,200 ft. Graded easy, it demands respect for Himachal weather, Leave No Trace camping, and honest self-assessment before you book.

Best time to visit: April–June and September–November for most stable Dhauladhar weather

Flora along the Aghanjar Mahadev corridor: Regional mix of chir pine, ban oak, rhododendron, deodar, and high-altitude scrub depending on elevation band.

Wildlife notes for Aghanjar Mahadev Trek: Himalayan birds (monal, griffons), langurs in forests, and occasional goral on steep grass — observe from distance.

Photography spots: Aghanjar Mahadev main viewpoint; Valley light at golden hour; Camp ridgeline panoramas; Village / temple cultural frames.

Camping locations: Not applicable — day trek.

Water sources: Carry adequate water from the last village; treat natural sources before drinking.

Network availability: Expect patchy or zero network beyond the last town. BSNL sometimes works in remote valleys.

Electricity availability: Charge devices in town before starting.

ATM availability: Last reliable ATMs in Dharamshala, McLeod Ganj, Palampur, Baijnath, or Bharmour depending on approach.

Medical facilities: Basic trail first aid; nearest substantial care in Dharamshala / regional district hospitals.

Permits required: Inner forest / camping permits as required for the specific corridor; included when stated in package.

Forest permissions: Follow Himachal Forest Department and local administration rules for camping, fire, and waste.

Safety on Aghanjar Mahadev: Stay on marked trails and follow your trek leader’s briefings. Turn back early if weather deteriorates — Dhauladhar storms build quickly. Never wander alone after dark near cliffs, snow patches, or stream crossings. Always follow your trek leader’s brief for this specific route.

Travel tips: Arrive in Kangra a night early when mornings start at the trailhead. Download offline maps; do not rely on trail network. Carry cash for village tea shops and emergency transport.

Responsible tourism on Aghanjar Mahadev Trek: Leave no trace — pack out all non-biodegradable waste. Use designated camping zones and toilet practices advised by your guide. Support local porters, mules, and homestays fairly.

Nearby attractions from this trailhead: McLeod Ganj, Bhagsu Nag, Kangra Fort, Bir Billing, Masroor Temples.

Nearby treks: Galu Devi Trek, Bagalu Nag Trek, Kareri to Triund Traverse, Kareri Lake Trek.

Child policy for Aghanjar Mahadev Trek: 8+ with guardians. Children are welcome on easier Dhauladhar trails when accompanied by a parent or guardian. Minimum age depends on the route (typically 8+ for short day hikes, 12+ for overnight alpine camps). Share ages at booking for route-specific advice.

Private departures: Private Aghanjar Mahadev Trek departures can be arranged for families and corporate groups subject to guide availability.

Group discount: Group discount available for 6+ travellers on the same fixed departure.

Booking and cancellation policies for India Holiday Destinations packages apply — see FAQs on this page for the current fee schedule.

Things to carry beyond the packing list: Layered clothing; Rain shell; Headlamp; Personal medicines; Reusable water bottle.

Things not to carry: Single-use plastics beyond reusable bottles; Heavy jeans or cotton-heavy clothing for multi-day wet weather; Unnecessary valuables and excess jewellery; Alcohol and recreational drugs.

faqs.question: What is the difficulty level of the Aghanjar Mahadev Trek?
faqs.answer: This route is rated easy. The Aghanjar Mahadev Trek demands easy effort across 1 day(s). Train with stair climbs and loaded walks; respect altitude above 4200 ft.

faqs.question: How long does the Aghanjar Mahadev Trek take?
faqs.answer: The standard package is 1D (day trek). Actual walking time varies with fitness, weather, and trail conditions.

faqs.question: What is the total trekking distance for Aghanjar Mahadev Trek?
faqs.answer: Expect roughly 5 km of walking across the itinerary (out-and-back or point-to-point depending on the route plan).

faqs.question: What is the maximum altitude on Aghanjar Mahadev Trek?
faqs.answer: The highest point is about 4,200 ft (1,280 m). Ascend steadily, hydrate, and tell your guide if you feel altitude-related symptoms on higher Dhauladhar routes.

faqs.question: Where does the Aghanjar Mahadev Trek start?
faqs.answer: The usual starting point is Near Gaggal / Kangra approaches. Arrive the evening before when the itinerary begins early.

faqs.question: When is the best time for Aghanjar Mahadev Trek?
faqs.answer: April–June and September–November for most stable Dhauladhar weather

faqs.question: Is mobile network available on Aghanjar Mahadev Trek?
faqs.answer: Expect patchy or zero network beyond the last town. BSNL sometimes works in remote valleys.

faqs.question: Do I need permits for Aghanjar Mahadev Trek?
faqs.answer: Inner forest / camping permits as required for the specific corridor; included when stated in package.

faqs.question: Is drinking water available on the Aghanjar Mahadev Trek trail?
faqs.answer: Carry adequate water from the last village; treat natural sources before drinking.

faqs.question: Where do we camp or stay on Aghanjar Mahadev Trek?
faqs.answer: Not applicable — day trek

faqs.question: Can beginners attempt the Aghanjar Mahadev Trek?
faqs.answer: It depends on the grade. The Aghanjar Mahadev Trek demands easy effort across 1 day(s). Train with stair climbs and loaded walks; respect altitude above 4200 ft. If you are new to the Himalayas, choose a guided departure and prepare with regular walks carrying a daypack.

faqs.question: How do I reach the trailhead for Aghanjar Mahadev Trek?
faqs.answer: Fly into Gaggal (DHM) or take a train to Pathankot, then road transfer to Kangra. Local taxis and shared jeeps cover the final approach to Near Gaggal / Kangra approaches.

faqs.question: What should I pack for Aghanjar Mahadev Trek?
faqs.answer: Carry layered clothing, broken-in trekking shoes, rain shell, headlamp, sunscreen, personal medicines, and a power bank. A detailed packing list is provided on this page and with your booking confirmation.

faqs.question: Is Aghanjar Mahadev Trek safe for solo travellers?
faqs.answer: Guided group departures are the safest option for solo travellers in the Dhauladhar. Trails can be remote, weather changes fast, and night navigation is risky without local knowledge.

faqs.question: What is the cancellation and refund policy?
faqs.answer: Cancellations 45+ days before departure: 25% fee. 15–30 days: 50% fee. Within 0–15 days: 100% of the trek cost. For weather or government restrictions, we try to offer a viable alternative; cash refund may not be available. Approved refunds are credited to the original payment method in 10–12 working days.

faqs.question: Are meals included on the Aghanjar Mahadev Trek?
faqs.answer: Trek-day meals are included as listed in the package inclusions (typically vegetarian). Confirm dietary needs when you book so the kitchen team can plan.

faqs.question: What medical facilities are near Aghanjar Mahadev Trek?
faqs.answer: Basic clinics exist in Kangra. For serious emergencies, evacuation aims for Civil / Zonal Hospital Dharamshala. Carry personal medication and inform us of any conditions before departure.

faqs.question: Is Aghanjar Mahadev Trek crowded?
faqs.answer: Popular sections near towns can be busy on weekends; early starts help.

faqs.question: Can I combine Aghanjar Mahadev with Triund?
faqs.answer: Many travellers pair easier Dharamshala walks with Triund or Kareri Lake on separate days. Ask us for a custom multi-trek plan.

faqs.answer: This route is rated easy. The Bagalu Nag Trek demands easy effort across 1 day(s). Train with stair climbs and loaded walks; respect altitude above 5800 ft.

faqs.question: How long does the Bagalu Nag Trek take?
faqs.answer: The standard package is 1D (day trek). Actual walking time varies with fitness, weather, and trail conditions.

faqs.question: What is the total trekking distance for Bagalu Nag Trek?
faqs.answer: Expect roughly 6 km of walking across the itinerary (out-and-back or point-to-point depending on the route plan).

faqs.question: What is the maximum altitude on Bagalu Nag Trek?
faqs.answer: The highest point is about 5,800 ft (1,768 m). Ascend steadily, hydrate, and tell your guide if you feel altitude-related symptoms on higher Dhauladhar routes.

faqs.question: Where does the Bagalu Nag Trek start?
faqs.answer: The usual starting point is Local foothill trailhead. Arrive the evening before when the itinerary begins early.

faqs.question: When is the best time for Bagalu Nag Trek?
faqs.answer: April–June and September–November for most stable Dhauladhar weather

faqs.question: Is mobile network available on Bagalu Nag Trek?
faqs.answer: Expect patchy or zero network beyond the last town. BSNL sometimes works in remote valleys.

faqs.question: Do I need permits for Bagalu Nag Trek?
faqs.answer: Inner forest / camping permits as required for the specific corridor; included when stated in package.

faqs.question: Is drinking water available on the Bagalu Nag Trek trail?
faqs.answer: Carry adequate water from the last village; treat natural sources before drinking.

faqs.question: Where do we camp or stay on Bagalu Nag Trek?
faqs.answer: Not applicable — day trek

faqs.question: Can beginners attempt the Bagalu Nag Trek?
faqs.answer: It depends on the grade. The Bagalu Nag Trek demands easy effort across 1 day(s). Train with stair climbs and loaded walks; respect altitude above 5800 ft. If you are new to the Himalayas, choose a guided departure and prepare with regular walks carrying a daypack.

faqs.question: How do I reach the trailhead for Bagalu Nag Trek?
faqs.answer: Fly into Gaggal (DHM) or take a train to Pathankot, then road transfer to Dharamshala foothills. Local taxis and shared jeeps cover the final approach to Local foothill trailhead.

faqs.question: What should I pack for Bagalu Nag Trek?
faqs.answer: Carry layered clothing, broken-in trekking shoes, rain shell, headlamp, sunscreen, personal medicines, and a power bank. A detailed packing list is provided on this page and with your booking confirmation.

faqs.question: Is Bagalu Nag Trek safe for solo travellers?
faqs.answer: Guided group departures are the safest option for solo travellers in the Dhauladhar. Trails can be remote, weather changes fast, and night navigation is risky without local knowledge.

faqs.question: What is the cancellation and refund policy?
faqs.answer: Cancellations 45+ days before departure: 25% fee. 15–30 days: 50% fee. Within 0–15 days: 100% of the trek cost. For weather or government restrictions, we try to offer a viable alternative; cash refund may not be available. Approved refunds are credited to the original payment method in 10–12 working days.

faqs.question: Are meals included on the Bagalu Nag Trek?
faqs.answer: Trek-day meals are included as listed in the package inclusions (typically vegetarian). Confirm dietary needs when you book so the kitchen team can plan.

faqs.question: What medical facilities are near Bagalu Nag Trek?
faqs.answer: Basic clinics exist in Dharamshala foothills. For serious emergencies, evacuation aims for Civil / Zonal Hospital Dharamshala. Carry personal medication and inform us of any conditions before departure.

faqs.question: Is Bagalu Nag Trek crowded?
faqs.answer: Popular sections near towns can be busy on weekends; early starts help.

faqs.question: Can I combine Bagalu Nag with Triund?
faqs.answer: Many travellers pair easier Dharamshala walks with Triund or Kareri Lake on separate days. Ask us for a custom multi-trek plan.

title: Kareri to Triund Traverse
summary: Ambitious high traverse linking Kareri Lake country with the Triund ridge — navigation, stamina, and big Dhauladhar exposure. From ₹9,999.
overview:
Kareri to Triund Traverse is part of India Holiday Destinations’ Dharamshala and Dhauladhar collection. Ambitious high traverse linking Kareri Lake country with the Triund ridge — navigation, stamina, and big Dhauladhar exposure.

Linking Kareri country to Triund is a navigator’s delight — high connecting ground, stamina tests, and huge Dhauladhar side-views.

The itinerary usually starts at Kareri Village and ends at Bhagsunag / Triund, spanning about 36 km across 4 day(s) with a high point near 11,000 ft. Graded challenging, it demands respect for Himachal weather, Leave No Trace camping, and honest self-assessment before you book.

Best time to visit: April–June and September–November for most stable Dhauladhar weather

Flora along the Kareri–Triund corridor: Regional mix of chir pine, ban oak, rhododendron, deodar, and high-altitude scrub depending on elevation band.

Wildlife notes for Kareri to Triund Traverse: Himalayan birds (monal, griffons), langurs in forests, and occasional goral on steep grass — observe from distance.

Photography spots: Kareri–Triund main viewpoint; Valley light at golden hour; Camp ridgeline panoramas; Village / temple cultural frames.

Camping locations: Designated Kareri–Triund camping zones; Backup lower camp in bad weather.

Water sources: Carry adequate water from the last village; treat natural sources before drinking.

Network availability: Expect patchy or zero network beyond the last town. BSNL sometimes works in remote valleys.

Electricity availability: No reliable charging on trail — carry power banks.

ATM availability: Last reliable ATMs in Dharamshala, McLeod Ganj, Palampur, Baijnath, or Bharmour depending on approach.

Medical facilities: Basic trail first aid; nearest substantial care in Dharamshala / regional district hospitals.

Permits required: Inner forest / camping permits as required for the specific corridor; included when stated in package.

Forest permissions: Follow Himachal Forest Department and local administration rules for camping, fire, and waste.

Safety on Kareri–Triund: Stay on marked trails and follow your trek leader’s briefings. Turn back early if weather deteriorates — Dhauladhar storms build quickly. Never wander alone after dark near cliffs, snow patches, or stream crossings. Always follow your trek leader’s brief for this specific route.

Travel tips: Arrive in Dharamshala a night early when mornings start at the trailhead. Download offline maps; do not rely on trail network. Carry cash for village tea shops and emergency transport.

Responsible tourism on Kareri to Triund Traverse: Leave no trace — pack out all non-biodegradable waste. Use designated camping zones and toilet practices advised by your guide. Support local porters, mules, and homestays fairly.

Nearby attractions from this trailhead: McLeod Ganj, Bhagsu Nag, Kangra Fort, Bir Billing, Masroor Temples.

Nearby treks: Toral Pass Trek, Waru Pass Trek, Snowline Trek, Kareri Lake Trek.

Child policy for Kareri to Triund Traverse: 16+ experienced preferred. Children are welcome on easier Dhauladhar trails when accompanied by a parent or guardian. Minimum age depends on the route (typically 8+ for short day hikes, 12+ for overnight alpine camps). Share ages at booking for route-specific advice.

Private departures: Private Kareri to Triund Traverse departures can be arranged for families and corporate groups subject to guide availability.

Group discount: Group discount available for 6+ travellers on the same fixed departure.

Booking and cancellation policies for India Holiday Destinations packages apply — see FAQs on this page for the current fee schedule.

Things to carry beyond the packing list: Layered clothing; Rain shell; Headlamp; Personal medicines; Reusable water bottle.

Things not to carry: Single-use plastics beyond reusable bottles; Heavy jeans or cotton-heavy clothing for multi-day wet weather; Unnecessary valuables and excess jewellery; Alcohol and recreational drugs.

title: Waru Pass Trek
summary: Serious Waru Pass crossing in the Bara Bhangal mountain maze — long logistics, high camps, and expert leadership required. From ₹27,999.
overview:
Waru Pass Trek is part of India Holiday Destinations’ Dharamshala and Dhauladhar collection. Serious Waru Pass crossing in the Bara Bhangal mountain maze — long logistics, high camps, and expert leadership required.

Waru Pass deepens the Bara Bhangal puzzle: long valleys, high camps, and leadership that must read weather like a sixth sense.

The itinerary usually starts at Bara Bhangal approaches and ends at Waru Pass & exit valleys, spanning about 70 km across 8 day(s) with a high point near 15,800 ft. Graded difficult, it demands respect for Himachal weather, Leave No Trace camping, and honest self-assessment before you book.

Best time to visit: July–September weather windows only; avoid deep winter

Flora along the Waru Pass corridor: Regional mix of chir pine, ban oak, rhododendron, deodar, and high-altitude scrub depending on elevation band.

Wildlife notes for Waru Pass Trek: Himalayan birds (monal, griffons), langurs in forests, and occasional goral on steep grass — observe from distance.

Photography spots: Waru Pass main viewpoint; Valley light at golden hour; Camp ridgeline panoramas; Village / temple cultural frames.

Camping locations: Designated Waru Pass camping zones; Backup lower camp in bad weather.

Water sources: Carry adequate water from the last village; treat natural sources before drinking.

Network availability: Expect patchy or zero network beyond the last town. BSNL sometimes works in remote valleys.

Electricity availability: No reliable charging on trail — carry power banks.

ATM availability: Last reliable ATMs in Dharamshala, McLeod Ganj, Palampur, Baijnath, or Bharmour depending on approach.

Medical facilities: Basic trail first aid; nearest substantial care in Dharamshala / regional district hospitals.

Permits required: Inner forest / camping permits as required for the specific corridor; included when stated in package.

Forest permissions: Follow Himachal Forest Department and local administration rules for camping, fire, and waste.

Safety on Waru Pass: Stay on marked trails and follow your trek leader’s briefings. Turn back early if weather deteriorates — Dhauladhar storms build quickly. Never wander alone after dark near cliffs, snow patches, or stream crossings. Always follow your trek leader’s brief for this specific route.

Travel tips: Arrive in Bara Bhangal belt a night early when mornings start at the trailhead. Download offline maps; do not rely on trail network. Carry cash for village tea shops and emergency transport.

Responsible tourism on Waru Pass Trek: Leave no trace — pack out all non-biodegradable waste. Use designated camping zones and toilet practices advised by your guide. Support local porters, mules, and homestays fairly.

Nearby attractions from this trailhead: McLeod Ganj, Bhagsu Nag, Kangra Fort, Bir Billing, Masroor Temples.

Nearby treks: Snowline Trek, Laka Glacier Trek, Indrahar Pass Trek, Triund Trek.

Child policy for Waru Pass Trek: 16+ experienced preferred. Children are welcome on easier Dhauladhar trails when accompanied by a parent or guardian. Minimum age depends on the route (typically 8+ for short day hikes, 12+ for overnight alpine camps). Share ages at booking for route-specific advice.

Private departures: Private Waru Pass Trek departures can be arranged for families and corporate groups subject to guide availability.

Group discount: Group discount available for 6+ travellers on the same fixed departure.

Booking and cancellation policies for India Holiday Destinations packages apply — see FAQs on this page for the current fee schedule.

Things to carry beyond the packing list: Layered clothing; Rain shell; Headlamp; Personal medicines; Reusable water bottle.

Things not to carry: Single-use plastics beyond reusable bottles; Heavy jeans or cotton-heavy clothing for multi-day wet weather; Unnecessary valuables and excess jewellery; Alcohol and recreational drugs.

Video and extended gallery placeholders are reserved for Website Management CMS uploads.

Fixed departure calendar and live pricing shown in the booking panel remain editable from CMS.

faqs.question: What is the difficulty level of the Waru Pass Trek?
faqs.answer: This route is rated difficult. The Waru Pass Trek demands difficult effort across 8 day(s). Train with stair climbs and loaded walks; respect altitude above 15800 ft.

faqs.question: How long does the Waru Pass Trek take?
faqs.answer: The standard package is 8D / 7N. Actual walking time varies with fitness, weather, and trail conditions.

faqs.question: What is the total trekking distance for Waru Pass Trek?
faqs.answer: Expect roughly 70 km of walking across the itinerary (out-and-back or point-to-point depending on the route plan).

faqs.question: What is the maximum altitude on Waru Pass Trek?
faqs.answer: The highest point is about 15,800 ft (4,816 m). Ascend steadily, hydrate, and tell your guide if you feel altitude-related symptoms on higher Dhauladhar routes.

faqs.question: Where does the Waru Pass Trek start?
faqs.answer: The usual starting point is Bara Bhangal approaches. Arrive the evening before when the itinerary begins early.

faqs.question: When is the best time for Waru Pass Trek?
faqs.answer: July–September weather windows only; avoid deep winter

faqs.question: Is mobile network available on Waru Pass Trek?
faqs.answer: Expect patchy or zero network beyond the last town. BSNL sometimes works in remote valleys.

faqs.question: Do I need permits for Waru Pass Trek?
faqs.answer: Inner forest / camping permits as required for the specific corridor; included when stated in package.

faqs.question: Is drinking water available on the Waru Pass Trek trail?
faqs.answer: Carry adequate water from the last village; treat natural sources before drinking.

faqs.question: Where do we camp or stay on Waru Pass Trek?
faqs.answer: Designated Waru Pass camping zones Backup lower camp in bad weather

faqs.question: Can beginners attempt the Waru Pass Trek?
faqs.answer: It depends on the grade. The Waru Pass Trek demands difficult effort across 8 day(s). Train with stair climbs and loaded walks; respect altitude above 15800 ft. If you are new to the Himalayas, choose a guided departure and prepare with regular walks carrying a daypack.

faqs.question: How do I reach the trailhead for Waru Pass Trek?
faqs.answer: Fly into Gaggal (DHM) or take a train to Pathankot, then road transfer to Bara Bhangal belt. Local taxis and shared jeeps cover the final approach to Bara Bhangal approaches.

faqs.question: What should I pack for Waru Pass Trek?
faqs.answer: Carry layered clothing, broken-in trekking shoes, rain shell, headlamp, sunscreen, personal medicines, and a power bank. A detailed packing list is provided on this page and with your booking confirmation.

faqs.question: Is Waru Pass Trek safe for solo travellers?
faqs.answer: Guided group departures are the safest option for solo travellers in the Dhauladhar. Trails can be remote, weather changes fast, and night navigation is risky without local knowledge.

faqs.question: What is the cancellation and refund policy?
faqs.answer: Cancellations 45+ days before departure: 25% fee. 15–30 days: 50% fee. Within 0–15 days: 100% of the trek cost. For weather or government restrictions, we try to offer a viable alternative; cash refund may not be available. Approved refunds are credited to the original payment method in 10–12 working days.

faqs.question: Are meals included on the Waru Pass Trek?
faqs.answer: Trek-day meals are included as listed in the package inclusions (typically vegetarian). Confirm dietary needs when you book so the kitchen team can plan.

faqs.question: What medical facilities are near Waru Pass Trek?
faqs.answer: Basic clinics exist in Bara Bhangal belt. For serious emergencies, evacuation aims for Civil / Zonal Hospital Dharamshala. Carry personal medication and inform us of any conditions before departure.

faqs.question: Is Waru Pass Trek crowded?
faqs.answer: Waru Pass sees far fewer groups than Triund — expect quieter camps and longer self-sufficiency.

faqs.question: Can I combine Waru Pass with Triund?
faqs.answer: Many travellers pair easier Dharamshala walks with Triund or Kareri Lake on separate days. Ask us for a custom multi-trek plan.
